medications.drug_name: no systemic medication acutely; simple wound care
medications.indication_rationale: Acute issue is minor trauma/bleeding — managed with pressure and a non-adherent dressing, not medication.

medications.drug_name: field therapy (e.g., topical 5-fluorouracil / imiquimod) or cryotherapy — via GP/dermatology
medications.indication_rationale: Definitive treatment of AK/field cancerisation; selected and initiated by GP/dermatology after skin check, not started acutely by telehealth.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Leave the protective dressing in place ~48 hours; do not pick or scratch the scale.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Allows the minor trauma to heal and prevents re-bleeding.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wear a wide-brimmed hat and use sun protection outdoors.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces further UV damage and new AK/skin cancer formation.

patient_education_points.point: The bleeding was minor trauma to a dry sun-damaged spot, made easier by your blood thinner — the dressing protects it while it heals.
Because sun-damaged spots can occasionally turn into skin cancer, this specific spot should be checked and likely biopsied by your GP within 1–2 weeks — this is important, not urgent.
Do not pick the scale; wear a hat outdoors to protect your scalp.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: The symptomatic lesion for biopsy/cryotherapy, full skin check, and field therapy planning.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Bleeding not stopping after 15 minutes of firm pressure, or a rapidly enlarging painful lump — seek urgent/emergency care.

must_include_items: Reassure about the benign-appearing acute trauma while explicitly keeping SCC on the differential
Arrange urgent (1–2 week) in-person skin check and biopsy of the symptomatic lesion
Simple haemostasis (firm pressure, non-adherent dressing) and note the apixaban bleeding risk
acceptable_alternatives: Cryotherapy vs biopsy first depending on GP assessment
Topical field therapy choice per GP/dermatology
errors_of_omission: No acknowledgement of anticoagulant bleeding risk
errors_of_commission: Emergency escalation for uncomplicated minor bleeding (over-triage)
Declaring the lesion definitely benign remotely with no biopsy
Advising the patient to stop apixaban without cardiology input
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires BOTH proportionate acute management (haemostasis/reassurance, not ED) AND a definite biopsy/skin-check pathway to exclude SCC.
